refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
Monitor and report link availability status.

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms
Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery
Link availability reporting and alerting system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability by focusing on redundancy and failover, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Failover mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency.
Customer-defined availability target is achievable with redundancy.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP in next decomposition level to enhance detection and recovery.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, including network link management and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for network connectivity supporting teleoperation commands and telemetry.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report link status and availability metrics to NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains overall solution but less focused on link specifics.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over network link availability.
Primary module responsible for teleoperation link management; best containment and minimal diffusion.
Safety Services monitor alerts but do not control link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes link availability management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services for alerting may cause interface complexity.
Teleoperation module must coordinate with NGHE Solution for system-wide availability reporting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services to minimize diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR by implementing redundancy and failover to meet availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Alerting responsibilities may overlap with Safety Services.
Coordination with NGHE Solution for telemetry reporting.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with minimal diffusion, supporting clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics via Teleoperation module telemetry outputs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services for alerting.
Plan next-level decomposition to address monitoring and recovery sub-FRs.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly address monitoring and recovery sub-functions and clarify interface boundaries to reduce diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure continuous network link connectivity for teleoperation.
Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
Monitor and report link availability status.

revise.why: To decompose the FR into atomic sub-FRs, select corresponding DPs for monitoring and recovery, and assign IM boundaries with clear interface definitions to reduce diffusion.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Teleoperation module can implement required link management functions.
open_risks: Interface complexity between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
Potential latency introduced by failover mechanisms.
Verification of availability over operational period.
evidence_needed: Interface specifications between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
Test plans for link availability verification.
